concepts: why virtual DOM avoids costly real DOM writes
keys used to identify list items during diffing
questionPatterns: How does React decide what to re-render when state changes?
Why does React batch multiple state updates together?
evaluationCriteria: explains diffing between old and new virtual DOM trees
connects batching to performance (fewer real DOM writes)
understands this isn't 'magic', it's a reconciliation algorithm

concepts: when to choose one over the other
questionPatterns: Why wouldn't you use useState to store a value that shouldn't trigger a re-render?
What's a practical use of useRef besides accessing a DOM node?
evaluationCriteria: correctly explains the re-render trigger difference
gives a non-DOM useRef example (storing previous value, interval ID)
doesn't confuse the two

concepts: empty array runs once on mount
questionPatterns: Why did your useEffect keep using an old value of a state variable?
When would you return a cleanup function from useEffect?
evaluationCriteria: correctly diagnoses stale closure from missing dependency
explains cleanup function use (unsubscribing, clearing intervals)
understands the three dependency-array behaviors

concepts: class lifecycle methods vs hook equivalents
componentDidMount maps to useEffect with empty array
questionPatterns: How would you replicate componentDidMount and componentWillUnmount using hooks?
What lifecycle phase would you fetch data in, and why?
evaluationCriteria: correctly maps class lifecycle methods to hook equivalents
justifies data fetching in mount phase
understands update phase triggers (props/state change)

concepts: controlled: value driven by React state
tradeoffs: validation ease vs performance for large forms
questionPatterns: What's the difference between a controlled and uncontrolled input?
Why might a large form use uncontrolled inputs for performance?
evaluationCriteria: correctly defines both patterns
explains re-render cost of controlled inputs on every keystroke
gives a valid reason to pick each

concepts: re-render implications when context value changes
not a full state management replacement
questionPatterns: When would you reach for Context instead of prop drilling?
What's a downside of overusing Context for frequently changing state?
evaluationCriteria: correctly explains prop drilling avoidance
identifies that all consumers re-render when context value changes
recognizes Context isn't ideal for high-frequency updates

questionPatterns: Why did you choose Redux Toolkit over Context for state management in your project?
What does Redux give you that Context doesn't?
evaluationCriteria: gives a real justification tied to their own project, not just textbook
mentions middleware/devtools/predictability as Redux advantages
acknowledges Context is sufficient for simpler cases

concepts: immutability handled via Immer under the hood
questionPatterns: Walk me through how you handled an async API call with Redux Toolkit.
How does Redux Toolkit let you write 'mutating' code that's actually immutable?
evaluationCriteria: correctly describes thunk pending/fulfilled/rejected flow
can explain Immer's role in enabling draft-style mutation syntax
references their actual project experience

concepts: React.memo prevents re-render if props unchanged
premature memoization can hurt more than help
questionPatterns: When would useCallback actually matter for performance?
Why doesn't wrapping every component in React.memo automatically make an app faster?
evaluationCriteria: correctly distinguishes useMemo (value) from useCallback (function reference)
explains React.memo's shallow prop comparison
acknowledges overuse adds overhead without benefit

concepts: keys help React identify which items changed
using array index as key can cause bugs
keys must be stable and unique
questionPatterns: Why is using array index as a key sometimes a bad idea?
What goes wrong in a list if keys aren't stable?
evaluationCriteria: explains index-as-key bug with reordering/insertion/deletion
understands keys should be stable unique identifiers (like an id)
connects this to reconciliation correctness

concepts: composing built-in hooks inside a custom hook
questionPatterns: Have you written a custom hook? Walk me through what it did and why.
What are the rules around naming and using custom hooks?
evaluationCriteria: gives a real example from their own project if they have one
understands custom hooks are just composition of existing hooks
knows the naming convention and why it matters (lint rules)

concepts: moving shared state to closest common ancestor
passing state and setters down as props
questionPatterns: Two sibling components need to share state — how do you handle it?
What problem does lifting state up solve?
evaluationCriteria: correctly identifies moving state to common parent
explains the problem it solves (state desync between siblings)
recognizes when this doesn't scale (leads to Context/Redux)

concepts: client-side routing without full page reload
questionPatterns: How did you implement protected routes in your authentication project?
What happens on a hard refresh with client-side routing, and how do you handle it?
evaluationCriteria: describes a real protected-route pattern (wrapper component checking auth state)
understands server needs to serve the SPA shell for any route on refresh
references actual implementation if it's on their resume

questionPatterns: How would you improve the load time of a React app with a large bundle?
How would you render a list of 10,000 items without freezing the UI?
evaluationCriteria: mentions code splitting / lazy loading for bundle size
mentions list virtualization for large lists
shows awareness of measuring before optimizing (profiler, bundle analyzer)

concepts: passing props through intermediate components that don't need them
solutions: Context, state management libraries, component composition
questionPatterns: What is prop drilling and why is it considered a problem?
Besides Context, how else could you avoid prop drilling?
evaluationCriteria: correctly defines prop drilling
names Context and/or a state library as solutions
can also mention composition (children props) as an underused alternative
